Products/Agentic Commerce
NEW Agentic Commerce platform · GA

When AI agents start shopping for your customers, will you be the brand they pick?

ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and a new wave of autonomous shopping agents are already comparing brands, applying promo codes, redeeming gift cards, and checking out — without humans in the loop. 99minds is the rewards layer built for this new commerce surface.

Read the manifesto → See it in action
// 01
Be discoverable
Make your brand citable by AI shoppers
// 02
Be assistive
Embed your rewards in your AI assistant
// 03
Be programmable
MCP-native rewards for any agent stack
A
Shopping Assistant
Live
U
Find me a hoodie under $80 with same-day shipping. Use any rewards I have.
A
I found 3 options. Northwind has the best fit and you have $42.50 in store credit + a 15% Gold-tier discount available.
→ 99minds.checkout.preview(brand="northwind", customer="cus_8KP")
Hoodie · Northwind Heavyweight$78.00
Gold tier discount (15%)−$11.70
Store credit applied−$42.50
Total$23.80
U
Perfect, buy it.
The thesis

Commerce just got a new kind of buyer.

For thirty years, we built websites for humans. Then mobile apps. Then chatbots. The next surface is here, and it doesn't look at pixels — it reads APIs, compares structured data, and makes purchase decisions in milliseconds. Your brand is either set up for it, or it's invisible to it.

700M+

People are already using AI to research purchases.

ChatGPT alone has over 700 million weekly active users. A growing share of them ask "find me X" before they ask Google. The question for every brand: when an AI is asked, does it know to recommend you — and does it have the structured data to act on that recommendation?

// OpenAI public usage figures, 2025
$4.4T

Will flow through agentic commerce by 2030.

Forrester, Gartner, and McKinsey all project that autonomous AI agents — not chatbots, not search assistants, true delegated-purchase agents — will mediate trillions in commerce by the end of the decade. The brands that ship MCP-compliant infrastructure now will own the early mindshare.

// Industry analyst consensus, 2025
0%

Of legacy loyalty platforms can be invoked by an AI agent today.

Antavo, Talon.One, Smile.io — they were all built before MCP existed. Their APIs are designed for human-built integrations, not for AI agents to discover, reason about, and call autonomously. 99minds is built MCP-first, with structured tool definitions, deterministic responses, and audit-grade logs for every agent action.

// Architecture analysis, Q4 2025
The three layers

Three ways AI changes your reward stack.

Agentic Commerce isn't one feature — it's three distinct shifts happening in parallel, each requiring different platform capabilities. We built for all three.

// 01

Be discoverable by AI shoppers

When a user asks ChatGPT "best gift card for a foodie under $50," your brand needs to be in the response. And the answer needs to be acted on, not just cited.

  • →Schema.org Product + Offer markup, AI-optimized
  • →llms.txt + llms-full.txt with brand-level fact files
  • →Structured data feeds for OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity indexers
  • →Real-time inventory + offer availability via API
// 02

Embed your rewards in your AI assistant

Building your own brand AI? Customers want to ask it: "what's my point balance? Apply my best offer. Send a $50 eGift to Sarah." All of that should just work.

  • →Pre-built tool definitions for 40+ commerce intents
  • →Authenticated customer context — no account-mixing
  • →Deterministic responses for receipts, balances, redemptions
  • →Drop-in components for OpenAI Assistants, Vercel AI SDK, LangChain
// 03

Be programmable for any agent stack

Don't bet on a single AI vendor. Be agent-stack-agnostic by exposing your rewards through MCP — the open protocol that any model from any vendor can speak.

  • →Native MCP server, hosted or self-hosted
  • →40+ exposed tools: gift cards, points, store credit, promos, wallet
  • →Per-tool scopes, rate limits, and full audit trails
  • →One implementation. Every agent platform.
// LAYER 01 · DISCOVERABILITY

The new SEO is AI Optimization.

When someone asks Claude "what's the best DTC coffee brand with a loyalty program in NYC," the model doesn't query Google. It synthesizes from training data, real-time search, and structured Product schemas. Brands without AI-optimized data are invisible.

99minds gives you the AIO layer most loyalty platforms don't even know exists yet — structured Product/Offer schemas, llms.txt brand files, real-time offer feeds, and entity resolution against the major AI knowledge graphs.

  • // 01 AI-ready Product schema generated automatically for every gift card, loyalty perk, and active promotion in your account. Indexed by ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews within 24 hours of publish.
  • // 02 Brand fact file at yourbrand.com/llms.txt with your tier structure, perk values, redemption rules, and current offers — written in the format AI engines actually consume.
  • // 03 Real-time inventory + offer feeds for OpenAI's product index, Perplexity Shopping, and emerging agent marketplaces. Your active promo updates in their results within minutes.
  • // 04 Citation analytics — see exactly which AI engines are recommending your brand, for what queries, and how those recommendations convert to bookings.
P
best gift card for a foodie under $50
Searching across: brand directories, Product schemas, real-time offer feeds, customer reviews, llms.txt fact files. Found 12 candidates.
Northwind Provisions ★ Recommended
★ 4.8

Folio Market ★ 4.6

$20–$200 · 8 categories · Email delivery only

Marquee & Co ★ 4.4

$50–$1,000 · 4 categories · 7-day delivery

N
Northwind Concierge
Powered by 99minds

You have 8,420 points and $42.50 in store credit. Here's what you can redeem right now:
Available Rewards
$25 Store Credit2,500 pts
Free Shipping (1 yr)5,000 pts
Limited Edition Tote7,500 pts
Studio Tour (Platinum)Locked
Send Sarah a $50 birthday eGift
Done — sent to sarah@hex.co with a "Happy birthday" note. She'll get an email + Apple Wallet pass. You earned 50 bonus points for the referral.
Receipt
$50 eGift to Sarah−$50.00
Charged to Visa ••4242
Bonus points earned+50
// LAYER 02 · YOUR OWN ASSISTANT

Build your brand's concierge AI. We do the boring parts.

Every brand is going to ship an AI shopping assistant. The question is whether your assistant can actually do anything — or just chat about it.

99minds ships 40+ ready-made commerce tool definitions for OpenAI Assistants, Vercel AI SDK, LangChain, LangGraph, and any MCP-compliant runtime. Drop them in. Your assistant can immediately check balances, apply offers, issue gift cards, redeem points, and process refunds-as-credit — with deterministic, audit-logged responses.

  • // 01 40+ pre-built tools spanning gift cards, store credit, loyalty (balance, redemption, tier inquiry), promotions (apply, list, simulate), and wallet pass operations.
  • // 02 Authenticated customer context. Every tool call carries the verified customer ID, so your AI never confuses Alice's loyalty balance with Bob's.
  • // 03 Deterministic outputs. AI is creative; ledgers must not be. Our tool responses are structured JSON with stable schemas — perfect for chained agent workflows.
  • // 04 Hallucination guards. The model can't "imagine" offers that don't exist or balances the customer doesn't have. Every value comes from the real ledger.
// LAYER 03 · MCP PROTOCOL LAYER

The Model Context Protocol for rewards.

MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the open standard from Anthropic that lets any AI agent — Claude, GPT, Gemini, open-source — discover and call tools across your stack. It's becoming the USB-C of AI integrations.

99minds runs a native MCP server that exposes your entire rewards platform as a typed, discoverable, authenticated set of tools. Drop our endpoint into any MCP-compatible agent and it immediately knows how to issue gift cards, query loyalty, and redeem promos. One integration. Every agent platform. Now and forever.

gift_card.create
Issue an eGift
loyalty.balance
Query points + tier
promotion.apply
Evaluate & apply best offer
wallet.push_update
Update Apple/Google pass
+
36 more tools
Full catalog in docs
claude --mcp 99minds
# Add 99minds MCP server to your Claude / OpenAI / any agent
$ claude mcp add 99minds https://mcp.99minds.com
 
# Discover tools (zero-config)
$ claude mcp list-tools 99minds
✓ gift_card.create Issue an eGift
✓ gift_card.balance Check card balance
✓ loyalty.balance Get points & tier
✓ loyalty.tier_status Tier benefits + progress
✓ promotion.apply Evaluate best offer
✓ promotion.simulate Preview a campaign
✓ store_credit.issue Refund-as-credit
✓ wallet.create_pass Apple/Google Wallet
... 32 more
# Now any prompt can invoke them
$ claude "Send Ada a $50 birthday eGift"
→ gift_card.create(
customer: "cus_8KPq2x",
amount: 5000,
recipient: "ada@example.com",
message: "Happy birthday!"
)
 
✓ Created gift card gc_8x2K9q
✓ Email sent · Apple Wallet pass attached
✓ Audit log: aud_4nL2pX
Universal compatibility

Works with every agent stack.

Native integrations with the major AI platforms — plus MCP support for everything else, today and tomorrow.

Common questions

What buyers usually ask.

Agentic commerce is the emerging shift where autonomous AI agents — not human users clicking buttons — discover, compare, and purchase from brands. It's already real: ChatGPT can browse and shop, Perplexity has a shopping product, Claude can use MCP-compliant tools to act on your behalf. Brands without a deliberate agentic strategy will find their offers invisible to a fast-growing share of the market. The cost of being early is small; the cost of being last is enormous.
Model Context Protocol is an open standard (created by Anthropic, now adopted across the industry) that lets AI agents discover and call external tools in a standardized way. It's becoming the universal interface between AI models and the rest of your stack — like USB-C for AI. By exposing 99minds as an MCP server, your rewards platform becomes instantly callable from any compliant agent: Claude, GPT, Gemini, open-source. One integration, every agent.
Not at all. Layer 1 (Discoverability) works whether or not you have an AI assistant — it makes you visible to ChatGPT, Perplexity, etc. Layer 2 (Embed in your assistant) is for when you do build one. Layer 3 (MCP) is the universal interface. Most brands start with Layer 1 today and add Layers 2/3 over the next 6-12 months as their AI strategy matures.
Three layers of protection: (1) authenticated customer context — every tool call carries the verified customer ID so agents can't act on the wrong account; (2) per-tool scopes and rate limits — your agent might be allowed to check balances unlimited times but only redeem rewards 5x per day per customer; (3) deterministic, audit-logged outputs — every agent action creates an immutable audit record with full inputs, outputs, and the reasoning trace. You always know exactly what happened.
The structural answer: AI never holds the source of truth. The 99minds ledger does. When an AI agent says "you have $42.50 in store credit," it's because it called store_credit.balance and the real ledger returned that number. The model can't invent a value because the value is structured JSON returned from an authoritative API. This is fundamentally different from giving an AI free-text access to your data.
Most teams have 99minds tools live in their agent stack within a single business day. Add our MCP endpoint to your Claude/OpenAI/Vercel AI SDK config, run our auth flow once, and you're done. Discoverability (Layer 1) is automatic for active 99minds accounts — your Product schemas and llms.txt files are generated and served as soon as you flip the switch.

The agents are already shopping. Be the brand they pick.

Talk to our agentic commerce team about your AI strategy, your MCP rollout, or your AIO roadmap. We've thought about this longer than anyone.

Book a strategy call → Read the docs
